---
title: "Quickstart"
jupyter: python3
---

This walkthrough mirrors the *ExPanDaR functions* vignette using the bundled `russell_3000`
financial-accounting panel.

```{python}
import expdpy as ex
from expdpy.data import load_russell_3000

df = load_russell_3000()
df[["coid", "period", "sector", "sales", "nioa", "ni_sales"]].head()
```

## Treating outliers

Winsorize the numeric variables at the 1st/99th percentile:

```{python}
analysis = ex.treat_outliers(
    df[["sales", "nioa", "ni_sales", "cfoa", "roa"]], percentile=0.01
).dropna()
analysis.describe().round(3)
```

## Descriptive statistics

```{python}
ex.prepare_descriptive_table(analysis).gt
```

## Correlations

Pearson correlations appear above, Spearman below the diagonal; significant cells are bold.

```{python}
ex.prepare_correlation_table(analysis).gt
```

```{python}
ex.prepare_correlation_graph(analysis).fig
```

## Extreme observations

```{python}
ex.prepare_ext_obs_table(
    df, n=5, cs_id=["coid", "coname"], ts_id="period", var="sales"
).gt
```

## Time trends

```{python}
ex.prepare_trend_graph(df, ts_id="period", var=["nioa", "roa"]).fig
```

```{python}
ex.prepare_quantile_trend_graph(df, ts_id="period", var="nioa").fig
```

## By-group views

```{python}
import numpy as np

ex.prepare_by_group_bar_graph(df, "sector", "nioa", np.nanmedian).fig
```

```{python}
ex.prepare_by_group_violin_graph(ex.treat_outliers(df), "sector", "nioa")
```

## Scatter plot with LOESS

```{python}
ex.prepare_scatter_plot(analysis, x="ni_sales", y="nioa", color="roa", loess=1)
```

## Regression with fixed effects and clustered SEs

```{python}
res = ex.prepare_regression_table(
    df,
    dvs="nioa",
    idvs=["ni_sales", "cfoa"],
    feffects=["sector"],
    clusters=["sector"],
)
res.etable
```
